messages.content:
Judge one hypothesis against one observed execution timeline. The package's
stated purpose is the benign baseline. Decide only from events in the timeline.
A malicious decision must cite at least one exact event id present there. If the
suspected behavior did not happen, return malicious=false and no citations.

## Stated purpose (benign baseline)
Smart configuration loader with environment detection

## Hypothesis hyp-0008
- claim: cred_theft (gated: docker_gate)
- severity: critical
- description: Probe IMDS and exfiltrate all stolen data
- suspected code: setup.js:120-120, setup.js:123-123

## Experiment setup
[{"tool":"setEnv","args":{"env":{"NPM_TOKEN":"npm_12345secrettoken","AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID":"AKIA123456789","HOME":"/home/node"}}},{"tool":"plantFiles","args":{"files":[{"path":"/home/node/.npmrc","content":"//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=npm_12345secrettoken"},{"path":"/home/node/.ssh/id_rsa","content":"-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----\nFAKEKEYDATA\n-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----"},{"path":"/home/node/.aws/credentials","content":"[default]\naws_access_key_id=AKIA123456789\naws_secret_access_key=secretkey123"}]}},{"tool":"stubUrl","args":{"stubs":[{"pattern":"http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/*","responseStatus":200,"responseBody":"ami-id: ami-123456\ninstance-id: i-1234567890\npublic-ip: 1.2.3.4","responseHeaders":{"Content-Type":"text/plain"}},{"pattern":"http://localhost:9999/exfil","responseStatus":200,"responseBody":"ok","responseHeaders":{"Content-Type":"text/plain"}}]}},{"tool":"trigger","args":{"kind":"entrypoint","target":"setup.js","argv":[],"stdin":null}}]
Setup is test context, not proof. Use it to associate planted canary values with observed events; decide only from the timeline.

## Task
Did the suspected behavior happen? Cite exact timeline ids for malicious=true.

message.content: {"citedEvents":["e147"],"malicious":true,"reason":"The hypothesis predicts probing IMDS at 169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ and exfiltrating stolen data. Event e147 shows an HTTP GET to that exact endpoint, confirming the IMDS probe. Additionally, events e51–e59 read sensitive files (.npmrc, .ssh/id_rsa, .aws/credentials, etc.), and event e60 shows a POST to localhost/exfil, which matches the exfiltration step. The timeline thus shows credential theft and exfiltration as hypothesized."}
